name: FoliageSwaying3D
shortDescription: Sway foliage of 3D objects according to the wind.
description: Sway foliage of 3D objects according to wind.
* Multiple sway types: **Grass**, **Bush**, **Tree Trunk** (dead tree), and **Tree Leaves** (more coming soon)
* Works with both **regular meshes** and **GPU-instanced foliage** for better performance
* Optional **gust system** with texture-driven gust masks
* Optional **visual tuning**: gradient tint, saturation/contrast, PBR tweaks
* Collision is not yet supported for GPU instanced objects but is planned.

* The mesh complexity auto-detection (`SwayResponse` parameter) is not perfect and can produce inconsistent sway intensity across assets. If you encounter overly strong or weak sways, experiment with the `SwayResponse` value to make your asset's sway consistent. Use the debug option to find the auto-selected value, then increase or decrease it.

* Foliage material auto-detection works well and can usually be left empty, but there are edge cases with multi-material objects. If you don't have access to the source file of your 3D object, you can find your object's material name using the debug option in object's _Foliage swaying behavior_.

* The optional wind gust texture can create natural-looking gusts and is worth creating. If not, one will be automatically created.
To make one yourself, use only black and red colors (black will be ignored and red will be the actual gust map). For the best results, create a tileable 512x512 image or download one of the examples below. [01](https://i.imgur.com/ZbtDo8o.png) / [02](https://i.imgur.com/XyR58TO.png) / [03](https://i.imgur.com/qbIKlyD.png) / [04](https://i.imgur.com/yxThP42.png)

![Gust map examples](https://i.imgur.com/VmcU9IB.png)

* Hot reload supports all basic behavior property changes, excluding Sway settings (`Material to sway`, `Sway type`, `Enable uniform sway toggle`) and `Distance culling`, `Debug output`, `GPU instancing`, and `Custom PBR settings` toggles. However, if PBR settings are enabled as you start the preview, changes to specific PBR values will be updated on hot-reload.

propertyDescriptors.label: Use color gradient
propertyDescriptors.description: Enables top/bottom gradient tint and color grading (default: off).
propertyDescriptors.name: UseColorGradient

propertyDescriptors.label: Enable uniform sway
propertyDescriptors.description: If enabled, all instances sway similarly. If off, each gets random phase variation (default: on).
propertyDescriptors.name: UseUniformSway

propertyDescriptors.label: Debug output
propertyDescriptors.description: Prints material/mesh and auto-scale debug info to console (requires object to be in the scene; default: off).
propertyDescriptors.name: DebugOutput

propertyDescriptors.label: Sway response
propertyDescriptors.description: How much the object is deformed by the wind. For low-poly models use small number around 0.5. (0-200; 0 = auto)
propertyDescriptors.name: SwayResponse

propertyDescriptors.label: Ignore UV map
propertyDescriptors.description: Uses world-height gradient instead of UV gradient (only when color grading is on; default: off).
propertyDescriptors.name: IgnoreUV

propertyDescriptors.label: GPU instancing
propertyDescriptors.description: Uses GPU instancing for supported sway types (better performance; default: off).
propertyDescriptors.name: UseGPUInstancing

propertyDescriptors.label: Fade distance start
propertyDescriptors.description: Distance where fade-out starts. Requires enabled distance culling (0-100000; default: 1200).
propertyDescriptors.name: DistanceFadeStart

propertyDescriptors.label: Fade distance end
propertyDescriptors.description: Distance where fade reaches zero. Requires enabled distance culling (0-100000; default: 1600).
propertyDescriptors.name: DistanceFadeEnd

propertyDescriptors.label: Custom PBR settings
propertyDescriptors.description: Enables custom PBR tuning controls (default: off).
propertyDescriptors.name: UseCustomPBR

propertyDescriptors.label: Use two sided lighting
propertyDescriptors.description: If enabled, both frontface and backface are lit. Useful for planes/cards (default: on).
propertyDescriptors.name: UseTwoSidedLighting
